> >
> 📒 Files selected for processing (14) > > * `ForgeTrust.Runnable.slnx` > * `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.Tests/TailwindCliManagerTests.cs` > * `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.Tests/TailwindWatchServiceTests.cs` > * `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.csproj` > * `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/Tailwind.Common.props` > * `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/TailwindCliManager.cs` > * `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/build/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.targets` > * `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/runtimes/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.Runtime.linux-arm64.csproj` > * `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/runtimes/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.Runtime.linux-x64.csproj` > * `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/runtimes/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.Runtime.osx-arm64.csproj` > * `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/runtimes/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.Runtime.osx-x64.csproj` > * `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/runtimes/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.Runtime.win-x64.csproj` > * `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/runtimes/Tailwind.Runtime.Common.targets` > * `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/runtimes/packages.lock.json` > >

📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough Adds an optional Tailwind integration: new Tailwind project (MSBuild/pack), runtime binary resolution, a background Tailwind watch service, a core ProcessUtils for running processes, RazorDocs/example integrations, CSS inputs, and tests. ## Changes |Cohort / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Project & Ignore**
`\.gitignore`, `ForgeTrust.Runnable.slnx`|Ignore Tailwind binaries and generated CSS; add Tailwind project to solution.| |**Core Process Utilities**
`ForgeTrust.Runnable.Core/ProcessUtils.cs`, `ForgeTrust.Runnable.Core/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Core.csproj`|New `CommandResult` and `ExecuteProcessAsync` for running/streaming external processes; added InternalsVisibleTo for Tailwind tests.| |**Tailwind Packaging & Targets**
`Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.csproj`, `.../build/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.targets`, `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/tailwind.version`, `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/packages.lock.json`|New project that resolves/downloads/verifies per-RID Tailwind CLI, packs binary into runtimes/native, and adds a RunTailwindBuild MSBuild target.| |**Runtime / DI / Watch Service**
`Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/TailwindCliManager.cs`, `.../TailwindOptions.cs`, `.../TailwindExtensions.cs`, `.../TailwindWatchService.cs`|Binary discovery (runtimes, local, PATH), Tailwind options, AddTailwind DI extensions, and background watch service using ProcessUtils.| |**RazorDocs Integration**
`Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.RazorDocs/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.RazorDocs.csproj`, `.../RazorDocsWebModule.cs`, `Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml`, `wwwroot/css/app.css`, `packages.lock.json`|Register Tailwind services, add project refs/imports, remove inline Tailwind and inline CSS, add Tailwind input `app.css` and reference generated `site.gen.css`.| |**Example Integration**
`examples/razorwire-mvc/RazorWireExampleModule.cs`, `RazorWireWebExample.csproj`, `Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml`, `wwwroot/css/app.css`, `wwwroot/css/site.css`, `packages.lock.json`|Add Tailwind reference/import, register services, replace inline CSS with Tailwind input; remove old compiled `site.css`.| |**Refactor: Process Execution Usage**
`Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.RazorWire.Cli/ExportSourceResolver.cs`|Replaced inlined process orchestration with `ProcessUtils.ExecuteProcessAsync` and adapted error handling.| |**CSS Inputs (docs & example)**
`Web/.../wwwroot/css/app.css`, `examples/.../wwwroot/css/app.css`|New Tailwind input styles: comprehensive docs styling and example UI/animation rules.| |**Tests & Test Project**
`Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.Tests/...`, `Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.Tests/packages.lock.json`|New test project with tests for TailwindCliManager, TailwindExtensions, and TailwindWatchService.| |**Lockfile updates**
`**/packages.lock.json` (RazorDocs, examples, tailwind projects)|Added `forgetrust.runnable.web.tailwind` project entries to lockfiles.| |**Deleted files**
`examples/razorwire-mvc/wwwroot/css/site.css`|Removed legacy compiled stylesheet replaced by generated output.| ## Sequence Diagram ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant MSBuild as MSBuild/Targets participant Project as Tailwind Project participant CLIManager as TailwindCliManager participant ProcessUtils as ProcessUtils participant OS as OS/Filesystem rect rgba(200,220,255,0.5) MSBuild->>Project: BeforeBuild/Pack -> ResolveTailwindBinary activate Project Project->>OS: Download sha256sums.txt & binary (if missing) OS-->>Project: Binary + checksum Project->>Project: Verify SHA-256, set permissions, stage to runtimes/... deactivate Project end rect rgba(200,255,200,0.5) MSBuild->>Project: RunTailwindBuild (input -> output) activate Project Project->>CLIManager: Resolve executable path CLIManager->>OS: Check runtimes/..., local dir, PATH OS-->>CLIManager: Path or not found Project->>ProcessUtils: ExecuteProcessAsync(tailwind -i -o [--watch/--minify], stream?) activate ProcessUtils ProcessUtils->>OS: Start process, stream stdout/stderr OS-->>ProcessUtils: Process output + exit code ProcessUtils->>ProcessUtils: Ensure cleanup (kill tree if needed) deactivate ProcessUtils Project-->>MSBuild: Generated CSS artifact deactivate Project end ``` ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 4 (Complex) | ⏱️ ~60 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - forge-trust/Runnable#80 — Prior change introduced RunCommandOrThrowAsync and local process helpers; this PR replaces that local logic with `ProcessUtils.ExecuteProcessAsync`. - forge-trust/Runnable#79 — Also modified ExportSourceResolver process-launch logic; the current changes refactor that area to use the shared ProcessUtils. - forge-trust/Runnable#77 — Similar scope: earlier edits to ExportSourceResolver/process orchestration that are superseded by the new ProcessUtils-based approach. ## Poem > 🐰 In build-time burrows I nudge the breeze, > > Binaries chased, checked, and tucked with ease. > > CSS sprouts local — no Node in the den, > > Watchers hum softly, compiling again. > > Hop, sprite of styles, and paint every glen.
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 4 | ❌ 1 ### ❌ Failed checks (1 warning) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------: | :--------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ⚠️ Warning | Docstring coverage is 29.73% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
✅ Passed checks (4 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :------------------------: | :------- | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The title 'feat: add Tailwind CSS integration for Runnable web apps (`#99`)' accurately and concisely summarizes the main change: implementing Tailwind CSS integration for web applications. | | Description check | ✅ Passed | The description comprehensively covers the PR's objectives, including automated binary management, zero Node.js dependency, hot reload support, safer defaults, and robust build logic, with clear sections on key features and affected components. | | Linked Issues check | ✅ Passed | The PR successfully implements all major requirements from issue `#99`: a reusable Tailwind extension package exists, at least two consumers (RazorDocs and razorwire-mvc) adopt it, the default path avoids npm/Node, and CI/build integration is provided through MSBuild targets. | | Out of Scope Changes check | ✅ Passed | All changes are directly scoped to implementing Tailwind CSS integration: new Tailwind project, ProcessUtils enhancement, consumer example updates, configuration, and tests. No unrelated modifications detected. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing Touches

🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `feature/issue-99-tailwind-integration`

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview Adds a reusable Tailwind CSS integration for ForgeTrust.Runnable web apps that builds CSS via the Tailwind standalone CLI (no Node.js), with MSBuild build-time compilation and a dev-time watch service. **Changes:** - Introduces `ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind` (options, CLI resolution, watch service, and MSBuild `.targets`). - Adds `ProcessUtils` to standardize external process execution + updates RazorWire CLI to use it. - Migrates RazorDocs and the `razorwire-mvc` example from the browser Tailwind script to generated `site.gen.css` and adds Tailwind input `app.css`. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 23 out of 24 changed files in this pull request and generated 9 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/TailwindWatchService.cs | Background service to run Tailwind CLI in `--watch` during development. | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/TailwindOptions.cs | Options for enabling Tailwind + input/output paths. | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/TailwindExtensions.cs | DI registration extension methods for Tailwind services. | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/TailwindCliManager.cs | Resolves Tailwind CLI path from runtime assets / local / PATH. | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/tailwind.version | Adds a Tailwind version marker file. | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/packages.lock.json | Lock file for the new Tailwind project. | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.csproj | MSBuild logic to download + checksum-verify + pack the CLI binary. | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/build/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.targets | Consumer build target to run Tailwind build before project build. | | ForgeTrust.Runnable.Core/ProcessUtils.cs | New shared helper for executing external processes with capture/streaming. | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.RazorWire.Cli/ExportSourceResolver.cs | Uses shared `ProcessUtils` for `dotnet publish` execution. | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.RazorDocs/wwwroot/css/app.css | New Tailwind input stylesheet for RazorDocs. | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.RazorDocs/Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml | Switches to generated `~/css/site.gen.css` instead of browser Tailwind script. | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.RazorDocs/RazorDocsWebModule.cs | Registers Tailwind services in RazorDocs. | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.RazorDocs/packages.lock.json | Adds Tailwind project reference to lock file. | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.RazorDocs/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.RazorDocs.csproj | Adds Tailwind project reference + imports Tailwind targets. | | Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.RazorDocs.Tests/packages.lock.json | Updates test project lock file for Tailwind dependency. | | examples/razorwire-mvc/wwwroot/css/site.css | Removes prior handcrafted CSS (now expected via Tailwind output). | | examples/razorwire-mvc/wwwroot/css/app.css | New Tailwind input stylesheet for the MVC example. | | examples/razorwire-mvc/Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml | Switches to `~/css/site.gen.css` instead of browser Tailwind script. | | examples/razorwire-mvc/RazorWireWebExample.csproj | Adds Tailwind project reference + imports Tailwind targets. | | examples/razorwire-mvc/RazorWireExampleModule.cs | Registers Tailwind services in the example module. | | examples/razorwire-mvc/packages.lock.json | Adds Tailwind project reference to lock file. | | ForgeTrust.Runnable.slnx | Adds the new Tailwind project to the solution. | | .gitignore | Ignores downloaded Tailwind binaries and generated `*.gen.css`. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

**Actionable comments posted: 11**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@examples/razorwire-mvc/Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml`:
- Line 12: The stylesheet link for site.gen.css in Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml
is missing cache-busting; update the <link> element that references
"site.gen.css" to include the attribute asp-append-version="true" (so the
generated file querystring will change on rebuild), matching the RazorDocs
layout pattern; locate the <link rel="stylesheet" href="~/css/site.gen.css"/>
and add asp-append-version="true" to it.

In `@ForgeTrust.Runnable.Core/ProcessUtils.cs`:
- Line 30: The XML doc in ProcessUtils.cs claims an InvalidOperationException is
thrown when Process.Start fails, but the method currently returns new
CommandResult(-1, string.Empty, "Failed to start process") instead; either
remove/update that <exception> tag or change the method in the ProcessUtils
class to throw an InvalidOperationException when Process.Start returns false
(instead of returning the CommandResult), ensuring callers still receive a
CommandResult only on successful starts or catch the thrown exception; reference
the CommandResult type and the Process.Start failure path and update the
documentation or implementation consistently.
- Around line 67-71: When streamOutput is true the StreamToLoggerAsync calls are
fire-and-forget and may not finish before the method returns; change the logic
to capture those returned Tasks (e.g., stdoutTask/stderrTask-like variables)
instead of discarding them, and await them in the existing finally block
alongside the non-streaming stdoutTask/stderrTask so all output is drained and
exceptions observed; update the branch that calls
StreamToLoggerAsync(process.StandardOutput, logger, LogLevel.Information,
cancellationToken) and StreamToLoggerAsync(process.StandardError, logger,
LogLevel.Error, cancellationToken) to assign their Tasks to local variables and
await them in the finally section.

In `@Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.RazorDocs/wwwroot/css/app.css`:
- Line 291: Replace the deprecated CSS declaration "word-break: break-word;"
with the standard "overflow-wrap: break-word;" in app.css; locate the rule
containing the "word-break: break-word" declaration and swap it to
"overflow-wrap: break-word" (optionally keep "word-wrap: break-word" as a legacy
fallback) so the selector uses the standard property.
- Line 148: Locate the CSS rule that contains the declaration "word-break:
break-word;" and replace that declaration with the standard property
"overflow-wrap: break-word;". Ensure the single declaration is updated within
the same rule (you may also add "word-wrap: break-word;" before it for legacy
browser support if desired), keeping the rest of the selector and rules intact.

In
`@Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/build/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.targets`:
- Line 8: The RunTailwindBuild MSBuild target currently always executes; add
Inputs and Outputs attributes to the Target named "RunTailwindBuild" so MSBuild
can perform incremental builds: use the $(TailwindInputPath) (and any other
input files like tailwind.config.js, project Razor/view files via wildcards) as
Inputs and the expected generated file(s) (e.g., $(TailwindOutputPath) or the
compiled CSS output) as Outputs; ensure you reference the existing properties
TailwindEnabled, TailwindInputPath and TailwindOutputPath (and add additional
wildcards for extra inputs if needed) so the target is skipped when inputs are
unchanged.
- Around line 21-26: The fallback chain sets _TailwindToolPath to a hardcoded
../tailwindcss without validating existence, causing CI failures; update the
PropertyGroup so each assignment of _TailwindToolPath checks Exists(...) (use
_TailwindToolPathWin and _TailwindToolPathNix explicitly) and only set the
platform-specific path when the file actually exists, and add an existence check
that fails the build with a clear error if no valid path is found; additionally
implement a new MSBuild target DownloadTailwindCli that runs before
RunTailwindBuild (use BeforeTargets="RunTailwindBuild") to download/cache the
appropriate binary and set _TailwindToolPath to the downloaded file so
RunTailwindBuild always has a valid executable.

In
`@Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind.csproj`:
- Around line 94-95: The build currently skips checksum verification when
ExpectedHash is empty (e.g., no entry for $(TailwindBinaryName) in
sha256sums.txt); after the FindChecksum step add a failing Error MSBuild entry
that triggers when ExpectedHash is empty (Condition="'$(ExpectedHash)' == ''")
and emits a clear message referencing $(TailwindBinaryName) so the build fails
if no expected checksum was found; locate where FindChecksum is invoked and
insert this Error check to ensure missing ExpectedHash cannot be silently
accepted.

In `@Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/TailwindCliManager.cs`:
- Around line 64-75: GetCurrentRid() currently omits a Windows ARM64 RID and may
drift from the MSBuild/.targets RID logic; update GetCurrentRid (and add a clear
comment cross-referencing the matching ComputedRuntimeIdentifier entries in the
.csproj and .targets) so that when you add support for a new RID (e.g.,
"win-arm64") you update all three places in tandem; also remove the stray
leading space before the _logger field declaration to match formatting
conventions.

In `@Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/TailwindExtensions.cs`:
- Around line 16-38: Make AddTailwind idempotent by replacing direct
registrations with TryAdd patterns: use TryAddSingleton<TailwindCliManager>()
for the TailwindCliManager and register the TailwindWatchService as an
IHostedService via TryAddEnumerable(ServiceDescriptor.Singleton<IHostedService,
TailwindWatchService>) in both overloads of AddTailwind (the parameterless and
the configureOptions variant); keep services.Configure(...) as-is for options
but ensure you import Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.Extensions so the
TryAdd* helpers are available.

In `@Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/TailwindOptions.cs`:
- Around line 14-22: TailwindOptions exposes InputPath and OutputPath which can
be null/empty and are passed into TailwindWatchService (ExecuteAsync), causing
malformed CLI args; add validation in TailwindWatchService.ExecuteAsync to check
string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_options.InputPath) ||
string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_options.OutputPath) and bail out (log a warning via
the service logger) or throw a clear exception before building the CLI arguments
so the Tailwind CLI never receives empty --input/--output flags; alternatively,
document that InputPath and OutputPath on TailwindOptions must be non-empty.
```

</details>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/TailwindCliManager.cs`:
- Around line 31-52: The code in TailwindCliManager that uses
typeof(TailwindCliManager).Assembly.Location to compute assemblyDir fails for
single-file deployments; replace that logic to use AppContext.BaseDirectory as
the base directory for resolving runtime and local paths (keep using
GetCurrentRid(), _binaryName and the same "runtimes/{rid}/native" and local
filename checks), update the log messages via _logger as before, and ensure the
path existence checks and returns behave identically when using baseDir instead
of assemblyDir.

In `@Web/ForgeTrust.Runnable.Web.Tailwind/TailwindWatchService.cs`:
- Around line 77-80: The exit logging uses result.Stderr which is always empty
when streamOutput is true because stderr is written directly to the logger;
update TailwindWatchService to either (A) accumulate stderr into a local buffer
while streaming and assign it to result.Stderr (so the final log can include
it), or (B) change the failure log to omit {Stderr} when streamOutput is enabled
and rely on the stream logs for details; locate the streaming handler and the
code that produces the ProcessResult (result) and implement one of these fixes
so the final LogError in the watch loop prints useful stderr content.
```

</details>
